titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 720

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=952 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 11:41:10.673791+00 | translation moa=953 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 11:41:10.66332+00

p.cl: Ranulfus de Bonkil Omnibus videntibus uel Audientibus has literas Salutem .

Sciatis me intui
tu karitatis et pro animabus patris mei et matris mee et pro salute anime mee et uxoris mee et omnium
parentum meorum concessisse . dedisse et hac presenti carta confirmasse deo et beato Cuthberto
et monachis de Coldingham terram in territorio de Ederham que iacet iuxta Edere et uocatur
Toddehalech . in liberam puram et perpetuam elemosinam . Quare uolo ut predicti monachi prenominatam
terram habent teneant et possideant ita libere quiete et honorifice sicut aliqua elemosina in reg
no scotie liberius quietius et honorificentius habetur tenetur et possdetur .

Hiis testibus . Patricio
filio Comitis . Patricio filio Ade . Roberto de Vpsetelingtona . Dauid Marescallo . Thoma de Gordona .
Matheo de Normanuill' . Iohanne de sancto Michaele . Willelmo de Veteri ponte . Stephano de la Warde
robe . Henrrico de Prendergest . Willelmo de Witeslade . Alano de Swinton' . Patricio de Preston' . Symone filio
Thome . Waltero de Paxtona . Andrea de Paxtona . Willelmo de Lumesdene et multis aliis .
Ranulf of Bunkle to all who view or hear these letters sends greeting.

Know me, moved by charitable love and for the souls of my father and of my mother and for the salvation of my own soul and the souls of my wife and of all my ancestors, to have granted, given, and by this my present charter to have made firm, to God and to blessed Cuthbert and the monks of Coldingham, land in the territory of Edrom which lies next to the [White]Adder and is called Todheugh, in free, pure, and everlasting alms. Wherefore I will that the aforesaid monks have, hold, and possess the aforementioned land as freely, quitly, and honourably as any alms in the kingdom of Scotland is most freely, quitly, and honourably had, held, and possessed.

With these witnesses, Patrick son of the earl, Patrick son of Adam, Robert of Upsettlington, David the marischal, Thomas of Gordon, Matthew de Normanville, John de St-Michel, William de Vieuxpont, Stephen of the Wardrobe, Henry of Prenderguest, William of Wideslade, Alan of Swinton, Patrick of Preston, Simon son of Thomas, Walter of Paxton, Andrew of Paxton, William of Lumsdaine, and many others.
